The core work involves manual labor and operation of machinery, which is not significantly impacted by cognitive AI technologies. While there may be potential for physical automation, the cognitive AI exposure remains minimal as the job lacks a substantial digital coordination component.

title: Testers and graders, food and beverage processing
rationale: Testers and graders in food and beverage processing primarily engage in physical and sensory evaluation tasks that require human judgment and presence. While AI can assist in data recording and analysis, the core tasks are hands-on and involve sensory assessments that are not easily automated by AI. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in administrative tasks, but the primary work remains largely manual and sensory-driven.

title: Camera, platemaking and other prepress occupations
rationale: Camera, platemaking, and other prepress occupations involve physical tasks related to manufacturing and utilities, with limited digital coordination. While AI can assist with some digital prepress tasks, the core work remains hands-on and physical, reducing overall AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have limited impact on these roles as the primary tasks are not centered around digital coordination or communication.

title: Photographic and film processors
rationale: Photographic and film processors primarily engage in physical tasks related to the development and processing of film, which involves hands-on work with chemicals and machinery. While AI can assist with some digital image processing tasks, the core work remains largely manual and physical, limiting cognitive AI exposure. The role is more susceptible to physical automation rather than cognitive AI advancements.

title: Motor vehicle assemblers, inspectors and testers
rationale: Motor vehicle assemblers, inspectors, and testers primarily engage in physical tasks related to manufacturing and quality control, which are less susceptible to cognitive AI automation. While AI can assist in data analysis and quality inspection, the core work remains hands-on and requires physical presence. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI systems have limited impact on this occupation's core tasks, though physical automation may pose a separate risk.

title: Assemblers and inspectors, electrical appliance, apparatus and equipment manufacturing
rationale: The role of assemblers and inspectors in electrical appliance manufacturing is primarily physical and hands-on, involving the assembly and inspection of products. While AI may assist in minor administrative tasks or quality control processes, the core tasks are manual and require physical presence. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure is low, although there may be a higher risk of physical automation through robotics.

title: Assemblers, fabricators and inspectors, industrial electrical motors and transformers
rationale: The occupation involves primarily physical and manual tasks related to the assembly, fabrication, and inspection of industrial electrical motors and transformers. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks or quality control processes, the core work is hands-on and requires physical presence and manual skill. Therefore, the cognitive AI exposure is low, although there may be some risk from physical automation technologies in the future.

title: Machine operators and inspectors, electrical apparatus manufacturing
rationale: The role of machine operators and inspectors in electrical apparatus manufacturing is primarily physical and hands-on, involving the operation and inspection of machinery. While AI may assist with minor scheduling or data entry tasks, the core value of the job is manual skill and physical presence, which limits cognitive AI exposure. The potential for physical automation exists, but cognitive AI exposure remains low.

title: Assemblers and inspectors of other wood products
rationale: Assemblers and inspectors of other wood products primarily engage in physical tasks related to manufacturing and quality control, which involve hands-on work with materials. The cognitive and digital components of their work are minimal, and AI tools have limited applicability in automating these tasks. The occupation may face displacement through physical automation, but cognitive AI exposure remains low.

title: Other products assemblers, finishers and inspectors
rationale: The occupation primarily involves physical tasks related to assembling, finishing, and inspecting products, which are not significantly impacted by cognitive AI tools. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks, the core work remains manual and hands-on. The exposure to AI is low as the primary value of the job is in physical presence and manual skill rather than digital coordination or communication.

rationale: Labourers in mineral and metal processing primarily engage in physical tasks related to manufacturing and utilities, which involve hands-on work with machinery and materials. The cognitive and digital components of their work are minimal, with little reliance on coordination, scheduling, or communication tasks that AI could automate. While there may be some risk of physical automation, the cognitive AI exposure remains low.

title: Labourers in metal fabrication
rationale: Labourers in metal fabrication primarily engage in physical tasks related to manufacturing and utilities, such as assembling, handling, and processing metal products. These tasks are largely hands-on and do not involve significant cognitive or digital coordination components that AI could automate. While there may be some exposure to physical automation through robotics, the cognitive AI exposure remains minimal.

title: Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing
rationale: Labourers in wood, pulp, and paper processing primarily engage in physical tasks related to manufacturing and utilities, which involve minimal cognitive or digital coordination. AI exposure is low as the core tasks are hands-on and do not significantly involve digital communication or coordination that AI could automate. While there may be some potential for physical automation, cognitive AI exposure remains minimal.

title: Labourers in rubber and plastic products manufacturing
rationale: Labourers in rubber and plastic products manufacturing primarily engage in physical tasks related to the production process, such as operating machinery and handling materials. These tasks do not involve significant cognitive or digital coordination that AI software can automate. While there may be some exposure to physical automation, the cognitive AI exposure remains minimal.

title: Labourers in food and beverage processing
rationale: Labourers in food and beverage processing primarily engage in physical tasks related to manufacturing and utilities, with minimal cognitive or digital coordination components. AI exposure is low as the core tasks are hands-on and involve physical presence, with little reliance on digital tools for coordination or communication.

title: Other labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
rationale: The occupation primarily involves physical and manual tasks in processing, manufacturing, and utilities, which are not significantly impacted by cognitive AI technologies. While there may be some potential for physical automation through robotics, the cognitive and digital AI exposure remains minimal as the core tasks do not involve digital coordination or communication.

title: Petroleum engineers
rationale: Petroleum engineers engage in a mix of field work and technical analysis, with AI potentially assisting in data analysis, simulation, and optimization tasks. While AI can enhance productivity in data-driven aspects, the hands-on and decision-making components in unpredictable environments limit full automation. Tier 2 tools may aid in report generation and data processing, but Tier 3 multi-agent systems are less applicable due to the complex, non-routine nature of the work.

title: Forestry technologists and technicians
rationale: Forestry technologists and technicians engage in a mix of fieldwork and data analysis, including tasks like surveying, mapping, and environmental monitoring. While AI can assist with data processing and analysis, the physical and environmental aspects of the job require human presence and judgment. Tier 2 AI tools may aid in data management and reporting, but Tier 3 automation is unlikely to fully replace the human elements involved in field assessments and ecological evaluations.

title: Conservation and fishery officers
rationale: Conservation and fishery officers primarily engage in fieldwork, enforcement, and monitoring activities that require physical presence and human judgment. While AI can assist with data analysis and reporting, the core tasks involve unpredictable environments and interpersonal interactions, limiting cognitive AI exposure. Tier 2 and Tier 3 AI tools may aid in administrative tasks, but the overall impact on the occupation remains low due to the hands-on nature of the work.

title: Industrial designers
rationale: Industrial designers engage in tasks such as conceptualizing and designing products, which involve significant knowledge work and coordination. AI tools can assist with drafting, modelling, and iterative design processes, enhancing productivity. Tier 2 AI (LLM+tools) can streamline communication and project management, while Tier 3 systems could automate routine design iterations and client feedback loops, increasing exposure to AI-driven changes.
